feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: BW to datasphere questions
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I come from a BW background, so please excuse my “old-school” approach!

We are starting a project in SAP Datasphere, and I have a few questions:

1. Can we simulate BEx exit variables (step 1 and step 2) in Datasphere? If so, where would be the best place to implement them – in the Analytical View or Fact View? Or should it be handled elsewhere?
2. We have a requirement similar to BEx with two structures, and another requirement involving custom cell-level logic. How can we manage this in Datasphere? Which layer should we handle this in? This is for cashflow reports that require a high level of customization, which I could typically do quickly in BEx.
3. Is it possible to implement a “rule type” concept in Datasphere? If so, how can this be achieved?

We are using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) as the reporting tool, and we prefer to use Datasphere as the main design concept. However, if you think any of these requirements are better managed in SAC,please let me know.

Thanks a million

regards

entry.title: Datasphere - Last Logon Time
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a table I can use in the Sys Schema to get a Datasphere's user last logon time?

entry.title: Issue with Large Data Extraction from SAP B1 via SAP DataSphere: Socket Closed Error
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'd like to briefly explain my current situation and the issue I'm facing.

I'm extracting data from a legacy system (SAP B1) using SAP DataSphere. SAP B1 uses SQL Server, and the connection, including the agent, is working fine, as I have successfully extracted smaller tables without any issues.

However, when I try to extract data from the OCRD table, which contains approximately 500,000 records, the process fails and returns the following message:
Runtime error occurred. See log for more details.
Group messages:
Group: default;
Messages: Graph failure: producer1 failed with the following error: Socket closed.
Process(es) terminated with error(s).

I would greatly appreciate any suggestions or ideas on how to resolve this issue.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: How to Automate Datasphere CLI using python
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement to automate the Datasphere CLI using python.

Example: As per the below blog via CLI it's manual activity.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/use-crud-opreations-with-sap-datasphere-command-line-interface/ba-p/13682690

Suppose there is scenario, we have 50 replication flows, i want to update some content in JSON file. Manual Activity takes more time. So by using Python we are able to do it but authentication is blocking for manual intervention. While automate each command it is asking some token code. To streamline this process, please suggest some steps to bypassing the authentication step through automation using Python.

entry.title: Datasphere source system assignment pointing to Dev after moving to Qual system for replication flow
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a situation where our replications flows are pointing to Dev systems after moving to quality systems.

with on prem we have BDLS which take cares of Mapping but in cloud solution what is the approach? 

anyone have faced similar kind of issues?

Regards,

Raj

entry.title: Advice on Reducing DHCDC_RTSTORE Table Size in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Good day everyone,

We are currently consuming SAP business content in our Datasphere environment, with data being replicated from our on-premise S/4HANA system via replication flows.

Today, my SAP Basis team informed me that the DHCDC_RTSTORE table in S/4HANA has significantly increased in size. They have asked if there's a way to clean up or reduce the space used by this table.

Does anyone have experience or suggestions on how to handle this?

Thank you in advance for your help

 

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: You are not authorized to query the remote system" in SAP Datasphere Tunnel Connection
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am trying to establish a tunnel connection between SAP BW/4HANA and SAP Datasphere, but I’m encountering the following error:

Spoiler
"You are not authorized to query the remote system. Please ask your administrator to grant you the InA role."

Could someone help me understand what this means and how to resolve it? I believe it’s related to missing authorizations, but I’m not sure how to grant the required roles or permissions.

 

Thanks in advance for your help!
Priyanka

entry.title: Datasphere & SAC - Decimal places in Export
entry.content:
Hello,

when i export a table to excel it gives me for some calculation many decimal places even though i set it to 2 decimal places.

For example

Data coming from  Datasphere via live data connection.

Is there any possibility to format this to 2 decimal palces?

 

Kr

entry.title: Anybody has access to SAP Datasphere basic trail for 30 days?
entry.content: I am getting the below message and I can't login

entry.title: Multiple entry option in Input parameters of Graphical, SQL View option in data builder data sphere
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to create multiple entry Input parameters in Graphical, SQL View option in data builder data sphere on Table. I don't see multi entry option for input parameters. Do we have any alternate way to pass multiple values through input parameters of Graphical, SQL View ? EX: Company code , i want to pass CC100, CC200 to the where clause of table/map it to the Company code field.

I appreciate all your inputs.  Thanks.

 

Regards,

Joe

entry.title: Unable to Retrieve Organization Secret and User Secret from Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi all, in an attempt to integrate SAP Open Connectors to Datasphere I followed the following guide to set up BTP and establish an Open Connectors Instance:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/extend-sap-datasphere-with-sap-open-connectors/ba-p/13554164

however I require an Organizational Secret as well as a User Secret which are not visible.

I'm not sure if there are role collections but I've following the following for access troubleshooting:

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configuring-user-access
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/grant-users-access-to-open-connectors-capability-within-sap-integration/ba-p/13574483

I was the first Integration Suite Provisioner in this subaccount.

Any assistance would be greatly appreciated

entry.title: Optimizing Data Load for Large Replication Flow Object
entry.content:
Good day All,

I have a quick question regarding the optimization of data loading for a replication flow object containing over 400 million records. The object in question is the CDS view I_GLACCOUNTLINEITEMRAWDATA, which includes 348 columns.

We are using replication flows to transfer data from an on-premise S/4HANA system to Datasphere, utilizing an initial + delta load method.

I started the flow on Wednesday, but it stalled and ultimately failed on Thursday night after loading 170 million records out of the expected 400 million. The flow was stopped and then restarted. It is now presenting the following errors in Datasphere and S/4 HANA

SAP Datasphere:

S4 (Transaction code DHCDCMON )

Does anyone have suggestions or experience with optimizing the load process for such a large data set?

Thank you in advance for your guidance!

SAP Datasphere

SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Error FWM 02151 Using SAP Business Objects 4.3. The token between Datasphere and BO expire.
entry.content:
A few months ago we connected sap datasphere with bo 4.3 directly without universes in between. For that we configure the token, which does not expire. But every now and then it happens to us that it blocks and the user cannot access the report. What is the reason and what is the permanent solution?

We provisionally found that by accessing bo with the user, we renewed the token and it works

Thanks in advance!!

Natalia.

entry.title: How can you model a Parent-Child Hierarchy with two Key attributes in the Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hello,

My issue is that I have two dimensional tables: Project and Costs. Each project has one or multiple cost codes.

Project

Pr_nrattributes
001x
002xx
003xxx

and Cost

Pr_nrCost_CodeDepBudget
001AFI10000
001BCO5000
002BFI8000

My problem is that some attributes can only be identified by the combined key of Cost_Code and Pr_nr. I am a beginner in this field, so my expertise is limited. I considered merging the dimensions and using the combined key as the identifier, but my problem still stands. Due to the combined key, I need to build a parent-child hierarchy.

Any help is much appreciated. Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Marco

entry.title: How to integrate SAP Datasphere Consumption API in SAP BTP Destination and SAP BTP-BPA
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing an issue when creating a destination on Sap data sphere Consumption API. I am getting a 401 authorization issue when creating a Destination using Oauth 2.0. However, using the same URL in Postman is working fine.

Can you suggest a solution to this issue?

This is the authorization I am using for Postman.

entry.title: Hierarchy not brought in via Cloud data integration connection in Datasphere
entry.content: When I tried to bring in the Master Data (from the Dimension Table) via the SAC Connection created in Datasphere using the Cloud Data Integration connection in Datasphere, the Hierarchy is not brought into Datasphere from the Dimension table in SAC Model. Other columns like Member ID and Description are brought in except the Hierarchy column. I tried via both the Remote Tables and Data Flow; the Hierarchy is not brought in from SAC to Datasphere using the Cloud Data Integration (CDI) connection. Can you support on how to bring the Hierarchy using the CDI connection.

entry.title: replicating S/4 VDM models in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

Given that there is a vast array of CDS views and Queries withing SAP S/4 VDM, is it necessary to recreate similar models in Datasphere?  It sounds like a duplication of effort.  What do you all think?  What would be the best practice in this regard?  As you know, SAP SAC provides live connection to S/4 for reporting utilizing the vast array of built-in CDS Analytical Queries.   There must be a blog on this subject, but I cannot find it.  Let me know if you know of any blogs on this subject.

Thanks

Abdul

entry.title: How to Replace 0 with Blank in SAP Datasphere and SAC
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m working in SAP Datasphere and trying to implement a solution where the "Subtotal Amt" field shows a blank instead of 0. However, even after applying a calculated column using the following formula:

CASE WHEN "Subtotal Amt" = 0 THEN NULL ELSE "Subtotal Amt"

The system still shows 0 instead of a blank value in Analytic Model and SAC.

I’m looking for a way to display a true blank (or NULL) for 0 values in SAP Datasphere, so that it also reflects correctly in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) reports.

What I’ve Tried:

  • Creating calculated columns with NULL.
  • Ensuring data model updates and SAC model refreshes.
  • Attempting conditional formatting in SAC, which did not solve the issue either.

Has anyone successfully implemented a solution where numeric 0 values are replaced with blanks? Any help or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Open Connectors with Datasphere "may not work"
entry.content:
Hello,

How can I know in advance if an Open Connector will work with Datasphere before buying the Integration Suite?

According to this note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003445022

Not all Open Connectors work with datasphere!!

And from SAP Help page page https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/9bfe7db51216449d985a0b59f5e181c4.html?locale=en-US

We can see that the data extraction is always done with a full retrieve of the source without filter/parameter push-down capability.

By the way, entering in the link "Working with Connectors".

gives no usefull answers https://help.openconnectors.ext.hana.ondemand.com/home/working-with-elements

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Improve Third Party Apps Data Consumption from Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have been experimenting long data retrieval durations when consuming views created in SAP Datasphere in Third Party apps like Excel and Power BI.

We consume data via ODBC Connection (changing this is not an option for us), so we use the Database user from each space we wish to consume data in order to make a successful connection.

Every graphical view or SQL view created, uses as data sources remote tables. We work through data federation as we cannot store data on the cloud due to security politics. The remote tables have as main source On-premise SAP HANA Warehouse, connected to Datasphere through a DPA (Data Provisioning Agent).

We have various cases, some of them, we identified the SQL query is complex and that's why it takes a long time.

But other cases, when the SQL Query is simple, but the number of rows is high (600,000) we suppose we should have a better time when retrieving that data (as with other tools the time is considerable smaller).

Is there any parameter we could modify in the DPA configuration, HANA configuration or in Datasphere, so we can improve the data retrieval times?

Thank you.
